checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-013 each specify concrete, measurable acceptance signals: exact HTTP method restriction (GET-only, no POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE), a 256 KiB truncation threshold with truncated/returnedBytes fields (REQ-008, AC-004), a fixed 7+3 error-code enum with explicit upstream-status mappings (REQ-006, AC-010/011), and static-check requirements for write-method absence, fs-write absence, child_process absence, and eval absence (REQ-003, AC-006/007). Every requirement traces to at least one AC with a named integration/unit/static test id and target path, giving a downstream implementer/verifier an unambiguous pass/fail signal.

All five Goals bullets map to ACs: read-only CI info retrieval -> AC-001/002/003/004/005/013/015; zero write-tool exposure -> AC-006/007; token non-leakage -> AC-008/009; upstream error/rate-limit normalization -> AC-010/011; installer distribution and idempotent registration -> AC-016/017/018/019 (README doc coverage AC-019). No goal lacks a corresponding acceptance criterion.

Each AC-001..019 states an externally observable outcome: specific envelope fields returned (AC-001-005), a grep-based static-analysis PASS/FAIL (AC-006/007), byte-for-byte dist-parity rebuild comparison (AC-014), stdio tools/list tool-count smoke check (AC-015), and installer file-placement/registration-entry diffs with HOME isolation (AC-016-018). None rely on vague intent language.

Non-goals section explicitly excludes write operations, artifact binary download (deferred to OQ-002 with an owner and resolution path), non-Actions GitHub features (explicitly deferred to github-mcp-server/sdd-forge-mcp), GHES base-URL handling (deferred to OQ-003), gh CLI token/exec usage, and knowledge-mcp/repo-mirror (separate phase/issue). Boundaries are concrete rather than open-ended.

Security Boundaries table (B1-B3) states auth/authz requirements, data classification (internal, upstream treated as untrusted), and regulatory constraint (GitHub API ToS/rate limit) per trust boundary. REQ-005 mandates env-var-only token intake with scrubbing from response/log/error paths. Assumptions section records the required PAT scope class and records that the read-only boundary plus its static verification is to be captured as ADR-0006 with explicit 'human approval' noted as required (人間の承認対象とする), giving an explicit approval boundary.

Risks section lists three high-risk claims, each tied to a concrete validation surface: write-tool/write-API leakage risk -> static GET-fixed check + negative tests (AC-006/007, TEST-006/007); token/log leakage risk -> mandatory canary-scrubbing test (AC-009, TEST-009); upstream API/rate-limit behavior drift -> fake-HTTP error-path tests (AC-010/011, TEST-010/011). The memory-exhaustion risk from oversized job logs is mitigated via the 256 KiB tail-priority truncation validated by AC-004/TEST-004. No high-risk claim is left without a planned validation path.
